response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a well-rounded software engineer with strong craftsmanship practices and experience at scale, but his technical profile is a poor match for this role. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript across recent positions at Stripe and Shopify, with no demonstrated Java or Kotlin depth, no JVM expertise, and no Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience anywhere in his history. His closest IDE-adjacent work was writing C/C++ components for NI LabWindows in 2014–2015 — a different language ecosystem and over a decade ago. Java appears in his skills list and briefly at Altom for peripheral tooling, but not at the depth or context required for enterprise JVM IDE development.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) demonstrated across multiple roles including Shopify and RIDE Capital
Developer tooling experience at Stripe (MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks) shows genuine interest in building tools for developers
Prior IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments (NI LabWindows/CVI) and a habit of organizing code katas and refactoring dojos signals affinity for developer tooling culture
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin proficiency at a level relevant to JVM tooling; primary language across recent and most impactful roles is TypeScript/Node.js
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework experience in any role or project
IDE internals experience is limited to a single dated position in an unrelated language ecosystem (C/C++, NI LabWindows, 2014–2015)

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling interest and craftsmanship practices
